---
title: "Research"
---

### Projects

Uprooted Democracy: Displacement and the Politics of Recognition

-   Examines why states recognize some forms of displacement (climate-, development-, and persecution-led) while rendering others legally invisible, and what this reveals about democratic quality in Latin America

### Publications

**Sinotte, Étienne.** (Under review; working paper available). "Making Collaborative Governance Work in Contexts of State Weakness and Disengagement: Peru’s Indigenous COVID-19 Commands."

-   Explains subnational variation the effectiveness of Peru's indigenous COVID Commands through pre-existing collaborative networks and NGO involvement
-   Shows how civil society can sustain coordination in medium-capacity states when the state disengages

Brouard, François, Natalya Brown, Manuel Litalien and **Étienne Sinotte**. 2025. [“Definitions of northern/rural/remote/peripheric communities in Canada.”](https://doi.org/10.4337/9781035338931.00012) In Research Companion to the Periphery and Peripheral Regions: Concepts, Diagnosis, Dilemmas, edited by Jerzy Banski. Edward Elgar Publishing.

-   Reviews competing definitions of northern, rural, remote, and peripheric communities in Canada, mapping four key continua (northern/southern, rural/urban, remote/close, peripheric/core)
-   Argues that relying on any single classification risks oversimplifying the realities of smaller communities, with consequences for welfare programs and philanthropic efforts

### Book Reviews

**Sinotte, Étienne.** 2025. [“Climate Displacement By Jamie Draper Oxford, United Kingdom: Oxford University Press, 2023. 272pp. £90.00/ US\$115.00. ISBN 978 0192 870162.”](https://doi.org/10.1093/jrs/feae087) *Journal of Refugee Studies* 38 (3): 813–15.

**Sinotte, Étienne.** 2024. “Recognition Politics: Indigenous Rights and Ethnic Conflict in the Andes: By Lorenza B. Fontana, Cambridge, Cambridge University Press, 2023, 250 Pp., US\$99.99 (Hardcover), ISBN 9781009265515.” *Canadian Journal of Latin American and Caribbean Studies / Revue Canadienne Des Études Latino-Américaines et Caraïbes* 49 (1): 117–19.

**Sinotte, Étienne.** 2023. [“Voice and Inequality: Poverty and Political Participation in Latin American Democracies, de Carew Boulding et Claudio A. Holzner, New York, Oxford University Press, 2021, 244 p.”](https://doi.org/10.7202/1106146ar) *Politique et Sociétés* 42 (3): 218.

### Media

**Sinotte, Étienne.** 2026. [“Ousting of Peru’s president points to a deeper crisis.”](https://doi.org/10.64628/AAM.jxeyj7ppm) The Conversation Canada. March 5, 2026.

### Grants and Fellowships

-   *2025-2026* Doctoral Research Fellowship from the Interuniversity Research Team on Governance and Inclusion in Latin America (ÉRIGAL)

-   *2024-2027* Canada Graduate Scholarship (Ph.D.; CGS-D) from the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC)

-   *2022-2023* Canada Graduate Scholarship (M.A.; CGS-M) from the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC) 

-   *2022* Discovery Scholarship from the University of Montreal’s School of Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies

### Affiliations

-   [Member](https://csdc-cecd.ca/etudiants/?id=697) (2025-), Centre for the Study of Democratic Citizenship (CSDC)

-   [Member](https://www.mcgillrefugeeresearchgroup.com/members) (2025-), McGill Refugee Research Group (MRRG) 

-   [Member](https://www.erigal.org/en_CA/le-profil/etienne-sinotte) (2021-), Interuniversity Research Team on Governance and Inclusion in Latin America (ÉRIGAL)

-   Visiting Scholar (2025), *Centro de Investigaciones Sociológicas, Económicas, Políticas y Antropológicas* at the *Pontificia Universidad Católica del Perú* (PUCP-CISEPA)

-   [Member](https://chaireparticipation2.odoo.com/etienne-sinotte) (2021-2023), Chaire de recherche du Canada Participation et citoyenneté(s)
